decision.headline: v5 IS SHIPPABLE via conformal abstention. v6 --run-train likely UNNECESSARY.

v5_frontier_recert.how: frontier certify (work branch 29d8a313) on olmoe-qat-spark-v5, cert-only --bench-b --execute, n=256.
v5_frontier_recert.rawVerdict: FAIL at full coverage: top1 0.9219 (<0.97), mean_kl 0.0364 (<=0.05 PASS).
v5_frontier_recert.read: v5 clears 0.97 answered-top1 by abstaining ~14% (coverage 0.86); margin separates argmax-flips well enough that abstention rescues v5 -> v6 NOT needed for 0.97-answered.
v5_frontier_recert.caveat: answered_top1 MEASURED selective accuracy (n_test=128), NOT a per-token guarantee; validate larger n_test. canClaimAGI=false.

v6_hold.status: HELD per cloud (frontier makes it likely unnecessary).
footguns_before_any_training: lora.ParamWrapper fails with lora_dropout!=0 -> fused-MoE needs --lora-dropout 0.
v6_hold.note: v6 never produced a capability result. If un-held: needs BOTH --target-modules attn-mlp AND --lora-dropout 0.

worldModelAblation: step 2 (A/B/C) NOT started (frontier decision was the gate). Needs the 70B judge lane. Next action.
guardrailsAck: canClaimAGI=false; lint_claims before commit; no candidate->adopted flip; no plaintext secrets; JUDGE_HOLD set+cleared; CODE untouched.
needsFromOwner: Ship v5 @0.86-coverage/0.982-answered (abstain ~14%) vs train v6? Or validate frontier @larger n_test first?
